title: Defamation / Malicious Falsehood

statuteRef.label: Time limit for actions for defamation or malicious falsehood

questions.label: Date of publication
questions.helpText: The date on which the defamatory statement was first published. Under the single publication rule (s.8 Defamation Act 2013), the limitation period generally runs from the date of first publication.

questions.label: Were there multiple publication events or online republications?
questions.helpText: If the statement was published on multiple occasions, or republished online in a materially different form, the single publication rule may not apply and each publication may give rise to a separate cause of action.

notes: The court has discretion under s.32A Limitation Act 1980 to disapply the 1-year time limit if it is equitable to do so. This tool cannot predict the exercise of judicial discretion.
The single publication rule under s.8 Defamation Act 2013 means that for publications by the same publisher, the limitation period runs from the date of first publication.
If the manner of publication is materially different, a new cause of action may arise. This tool does not calculate multiple-publication scenarios in v1.
